data.body:
Additional context from host project debugging session:

The root cause is in record-test-status.sh line ~459. When a .ts file is encountered, it falls through to the else branch which runs `bash "$full_test_path"` — attempting to execute a TypeScript test file as a bash script. There is no Jest/Node handler for .ts files.

The RECORD_TEST_STATUS_RUNNER env var workaround also fails because the script treats the entire value as a single command name (e.g., `npx jest --passWithNoTests` becomes a single string passed to bash, causing 'command not found'). The runner value needs to be split so the first word is the command and remaining words are arguments.

Observed in parity-scan host project (Next.js/Jest stack).
